description: Revolutionary Council nationalizes private commercial banks along with special banks, insurance companies, and many other industrial firms
entity_names: all private banks except Bank Iran o Rus
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica BANKING i (Basseer), citing Bank Markazi Iran Annual Report 1980 (publ. 1982) pp.81-82

description: Contemporaneous Western press reports the takeover as occurring around June 8-9 1979 (minor date variance from the Iranica-cited May 28 decree date, likely reflecting the gap between decree issuance and public/international reporting)
source: Washington Post archive headline 'Iran Announces Takeover of All Private Banks' (dated in archive index as 1979-06-09); Iran1400.org gives June 8 1979

description: 36 banks (excluding Bank Markazi) operating in Iran immediately before nationalization; 28 were privately owned (13 of which were joint ventures with foreign banks) and accounted for nearly half of banking operations
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica BANKING i; Wikipedia Banking and insurance in Iran

description: Governor of Bank Markazi (central bank) executed following the revolution
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica BANKING i (Basseer), concluding paragraph

description: Plan for merging/consolidating nationalized banks with similar activities approved
Bank Ma'dan va San'at (Bank of Industry and Mining) created by merger of multiple nationalized banks
Bank Maskan (Housing Bank) created by merger of multiple nationalized banks
Bank Keshavarzi (Agricultural Bank) created by merger of multiple nationalized banks
Bank Tejarat (Mercantile Bank) formed via merger of eleven private Iranian, foreign, and multinational banks
Bank Mellat (People's Bank) formed via merger of ten banks
Bank Melli, Bank Sepah, Bank Refah-e Kargaran, and Bank Saderat continued operating under government control (not merged into the five new consolidated banks)
entity_names: Bank Melli / Bank Sepah / Bank Refah-e Kargaran / Bank Saderat
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica BANKING i, citing Bank Markazi Iran Annual Report 1980

description: Bank Iran o Rus (Iran-Soviet joint bank) was the sole bank NOT nationalized in 1979
By 1982 the consolidation had reduced the banking system to nine banks (six commercial and three specialized) and 6,581 branches, down from 36 banks/8,275 branches in 1977
column6: Wikipedia Banking and insurance in Iran, citing standard secondary compilation

description: 28 banks, 16 deposit-and-housing-loan companies, and 2 investment companies were taken over as part of the 1979-80 nationalization wave
source: Parstimes.com 'A Review of History of Banking System in Iran'

description: Majlis approves the Law for Usury-Free (Islamic) Banking, formally launching interest-free banking practice
Interest-free banking code fully implemented across the nationalized banking system
